app.name: AQBot
app.one_liner: A lightweight, high-performance cross-platform AI dialogue client and local AI gateway supporting multi-LLM providers, conversation branching, RAG, MCP protocol, and built-in API server.

screens.name: Local AI Gateway Dashboard
screens.purpose: Monitor and manage the built-in API gateway including usage stats, request logs, SSL settings, and client configurations.

screens.name: Main Chat Interface
screens.purpose: Primary workspace for conducting AI conversations with support for message versioning, branching, context management, and rich output rendering (code, math, diagrams).

screens.name: Settings & Providers
screens.purpose: Configure LLM providers, API keys, model parameters, system presets, and gateway settings.

rust_commands.name: compress_conversation
rust_commands.purpose: Apply automatic summarization to reduce conversation length while preserving key information for context window efficiency.

rust_commands.name: fetch_models_list
rust_commands.purpose: Retrieve available models from a configured LLM provider via its API.

rust_commands.name: save_conversation
rust_commands.purpose: Persist current conversation state including messages, branches, versions, and context attachments to local storage.

rust_commands.name: validate_api_key
rust_commands.purpose: Verify API key validity for a given LLM provider by making a test request to its endpoint.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add local storage for conversations and messages
week 1: Implement core Tauri app shell with main chat screen
week 1: Integrate basic OpenAI-compatible API calling with streaming
week 1: Support single-provider configuration in settings
week 2: Add Monaco editor for code blocks and diff preview
week 2: Build basic gateway server exposing OpenAI-compatible endpoint
week 2: Enable multi-provider support with key rotation
week 2: Implement message versioning and branching UI
week 3: Add usage tracking and request logging for gateway
week 3: Implement MCP protocol over stdio with tool execution panel
week 3: Integrate RAG with local document upload and indexing
week 3: Support Mermaid and D2 diagram rendering
acceptance_tests: Creating a conversation branch from any message node preserves full context and allows independent continuation.
Gateway-generated API key can be used by external CLI tools (e.g., curl) to query local AQBot endpoint.
Long model responses (>10k tokens) render fully without blank areas or truncation.
Non-OpenAI models (e.g., Claude) successfully perform web search without returning OpenAI-specific errors.
User can switch between OpenAI, Claude, and Gemini providers and send messages without crash.
Window does not minimize when external screenshot shortcuts (e.g., Feishu) are triggered.
